mentions.note: Best-in-class global off-price retailer; 5% same-store sales growth. OLLI comparison: TJX 30x earnings, OLLI 20x — deserved discount but OLLI opportunity growing.
Trade-down retail superb in war environment; Cramer owns for Chapel Trust; can access inventory from overburdened retailers; ideal recession hedge now getting cheaper.
6% SSS (vs. 4.1% expected), 9% revenue growth, 29% earnings growth, 17¢ beat; raised guidance and $250M buyback; HomeGoods +9%; off-price kingpin thrives when consumer nervous.
Still winning while other bargain retailers sink. Different model based on selection (sourcing full-price closeouts) not price point—continues to work in any economic environment.
Cramer's long-term favorite in off-price retail; mentioned as beneficiary of same discount-retail thesis driving Ross success. Would love to own both if diversification rules allowed.
Ideal place to shop for value in uncertain market; held up very nicely today. Travel Trust core holding showing resilience.
Value-oriented apparel/home goods retailer; reports tomorrow; tangible product focus; attractive rotation candidate.
Had a terrific quarter with HomeGoods, Marshalls performing well; stock hit despite good results but fundamentals remain strong.
Trade-down retail theme; HomeGoods, Marshalls, TJ Maxx divisions benefit as consumer shifts down.
MVP brand; everybody loves bargain business model. Value-for-price offering drives faster growth and multiple expansion.
Cleaning up via scavenger strategy on distressed retail inventory; extraordinary numbers across T.J. Maxx, Marshalls, HomeGoods.
Off-price retailer down 2.6%; had been phenomenal performer; decline paints nasty consumer picture.
